name: unbearable-lightness
description: Portia de Rossi's searing memoir about her decade-long struggle with anorexia and bulimia. A raw, unflinching account of the eating disorder that nearly killed her, her journey through Hollywood's impossible beauty standards, and her slow, painful recovery. With a foreword by her wife, Ellen DeGeneres.
useCase: A candid and courageous memoir about eating disorders, body image, the pressure of Hollywood beauty standards, and the journey toward self-acceptance.
knownRisks: Contains graphic descriptions of anorexia, bulimia, and self-harm that may be triggering for readers with eating disorders.
The book details specific caloric restriction and purging methods that could be used as instruction by someone with an eating disorder.
De Rossi's recovery narrative may not reflect all recovery experiences.
